title: Uplift
desc: Campus fitness app for Cornell's gym facilities.
body: Uplift gives Cornell students a single place to check gym hours, live equipment capacity, and fitness class schedules across all campus facilities. The app serves thousands of active users each semester and is maintained by a cross-functional team of engineers and PMs at Cornell AppDev. I joined as an Associate PM, working across the iOS and backend surfaces to prioritize and ship iterative improvements.

highlight.label: Impact
highlight.text: Thousands of active Cornell students rely on Uplift each semester to plan their workouts — replace this with your real metric.

challenges: Aggregating real-time occupancy and schedule data from disparate, unreliable gym data sources
Coordinating feature work across iOS and backend teams on a semester-based release cycle
Balancing new feature requests against stability and existing user expectations

title: CHEM
desc: Endowment management platform for a Cornell Hack4Impact nonprofit client.
body: CHEM is a fullstack web application built for a nonprofit client through Cornell Hack4Impact. It streamlines how the organization tracks endowment funds, models allocation scenarios, and maintains compliance reporting. The system replaces a manual, spreadsheet-based workflow with a structured dashboard that makes fund performance and regulatory requirements visible at a glance.

highlight.label: Client impact
highlight.text: Replaced a manual spreadsheet workflow — add the specific outcome or time saved here.

challenges: Modeling complex fund allocation logic that matched the client's existing mental model
Designing a data schema flexible enough to accommodate evolving compliance requirements
Shipping a production-ready product on a fixed semester timeline with a rotating team

title: Portfolio
body: A hand-built static portfolio site with no frameworks — just HTML, CSS, and vanilla JS. The goal was full creative control over every animation, interaction, and layout decision. Features include a 3D carousel on the projects page, a scroll-based reveal system, a filterable icon grid on the toolbox page, and a theme toggle with localStorage persistence.

highlight.label: Design goal
highlight.text: Zero dependencies, zero build step — just files served directly. Fast, auditable, and fully owned.

challenges: Building a smooth physics-based 3D carousel with drag inertia using only the Canvas/rAF loop — no libraries
Designing a consistent visual system (type scale, color tokens, spacing) from scratch
Getting smooth reveal animations without layout thrash across varying scroll speeds
